provisions.title: The purpose of this sub-decree is to regulate the solid waste
provisions.content:
management with proper technical manner and safe way in order to ensure
the protection of human health and the conservation of bio-diversity.

provisions.title: This sub-decree applies to all activities related to disposal, storage,
provisions.content: collection, transport, recycling, dumping of garbage and hazardous waste.

provisions.title: Technical term used in this sub-decree shall have the meaning ascribed
provisions.content:
there to :

a- Solid waste refers to hard objects, hard substances, products or refuse
which are useless, disposed of;

b- Household waste is the part of solid waste which does not contain
toxin or hazardous substance, and is discarded from dwellings, public
buildings, factory, market, hotel, business building, restaurant,
transport, recreation site,....etc;

c- Hazardous waste refers to radioactivity substances, explosive
substances, toxic substances, inflammable substances, pathogenic
substances, irritating substances, corrosive substances, oxidizing
substances, or other chemical substances which may cause the danger
to human (health) and animal or damage plants, public property and
the environment. The hazardous waste may be generated from
dwelling houses, industries, agricultural activities, business and service
activities, mining.......etc. The type of hazardous waste is listed in the
Annex of this sub-decree.

-2-

25-JUL-2002 THU 14:33 P, 01

Doc 8
Unofficial Translation

provisions.title: The Ministry of Environment shall establish guidelines on disposal,
provisions.content:
collection, transport, storage, recycling, minimizing, and dumping of
household waste in provinces and cities in order to ensure the management
of household waste with safe way.

The authorities of the provinces and cities shall establish the waste
management plan in their province and city for short, medium and
long- term.

provisions.title: The collection, transport, recycling, minimizing and dumping of waste
provisions.content:
in the provinces and cities is the responsibility of the authorities of
province and cities.

The Implementation as mentioned in the first paragraph of the article 5
shall comply with the guideline on the sound management of waste
specified by the Prakas (declaration) of the Ministry of Environment

provisions.title: The Ministry of Environment shall monitor the implementation in
provisions.content:
disposal, collection, transport, storage, recycling, minimizing and dumping
of the household waste in the provinces and cities.

provisions.title: The disposal of waste in public sites or anywhere that is not allowed
provisions.content: by the authorities shall be strictly prohibited.

provisions.title: The domestic investment in construction of landfill, incinerator,
provisions.content:
storage sites or recycling plant for household waste shall be subject to
prior approval from the Ministry of Environment.

provisions.title: The exportation of the household waste from the Kingdom of
provisions.content:
Cambodia to abroad could not be conducted unless there are approval
from the Ministry of Environment, and export license from the Ministry of
Trade, and permit from the import country.

provisions.title: The importation of the household waste from abroad to the
provisions.content:
Kingdom of Cambodia shall be strictly prohibited.

-3-

25-JUL-2002 THU 14:33 P, 01

Doc 8
Unofficial Translation

provisions.title: The Ministry of Environment shall establish guidelines on the
provisions.content: management of hazardous waste to ensure the safe management.

provisions.title: The Ministry of Environment shall issue Prakas on the standard of
provisions.content:
quantity of toxin or hazardous substances contained in the hazardous
waste which could be allowed to be disposal in order to ensure the human
health and environment quality protection, and bio-diversity conservation.

provisions.title: The owner of the hazardous waste shall be responsible for temporary
provisions.content: storage of his/her waste in proper technique and in safe manner.

provisions.title: The owner of the hazardous waste shall make quarterly report on
provisions.content:
his/her waste and forward it to the Ministry of Environment.
The report include:
- Type and amount of the waste,
- Temporary storage method, and
- Treatment or elimination method.

provisions.title: The storage, transportation and disposal of the hazardous waste shall be
provisions.content:
performed separately from the household waste, which will be stipulated
by the Prakas of the Ministry of Environment.
The disposal of hazardous waste into public sites, public drainage
systems, public waste area, and rural area and forest area shall be strictly
prohibited.

provisions.title: The collection, transportation, storage and disposal of the hazardous
provisions.content:
waste from dwelling houses, markets, clinics, hospitals, hotels, restaurants,
and public buildings are competence of the local authorities.

The implementation of the first paragraph of the article 16 shall be in
accordance with the Prakas of the Ministry of Environment on the
guidelines on the environmentally sound management of hazardous waste.

provisions.title: The transportation or construction of storage place or landfill of
provisions.content:
hazardous waste from factories and manufacturing site shall be subject to
permit from the Ministry of Environment.

-4-

25-JUL-2002 THU 14:33 P, 01

provisions.title: The owner or responsible person of storage place or landfill of the
provisions.content:
hazardous waste shall make quarterly report on the hazardous waste
which is transported for disposal of or for storage and forward this report
to the Ministry of Environment. The report shall include as follows:
- Type and amount of the waste,
- Sources of the waste,
- Packing and transport facility, and
- Process and management of the waste inside his/her location
and forward this form to the Ministry of Environment.

provisions.title: The investment of the treatment or incineration of the hazardous waste
provisions.content: shall have prior approval from the Ministry of Environment.

provisions.title: The exportation of the hazardous waste from the Kingdom of
provisions.content:
Cambodia to abroad could be conducted if there are an agreement from the
Ministry of Environment, export license from the Ministry of Trade, and
permit from the import country.
The exportation of the hazardous shall be consistent with the provisions
and principals of the Basel Convention on the Control of Transboundary
Movements of Hazardous Wastes and their Disposal in 1989 which come
into force on May 05, 1992.

provisions.title: The importation of hazardous waste from abroad into the Kingdom of
provisions.content:
Cambodia is strictly prohibited.

-5-

25-JUL-2002 THU 14:33 P, 01

Doc 8
Unofficial Translation

provisions.title: The monitoring of packing, storage, transport, recycling, incinerating,
provisions.content:
treatment, and disposal of the hazardous waste is the responsibility of the
Ministry of Environment.

provisions.title: The Ministry of Environment shall take sample of the hazardous waste
provisions.content:
at every point enumerated in the article 22 of this sub-decree.
The owner or person responsible for the site mentioned in the
paragraph one of article 23 of this sub-decree shall collaborate and
facilitate the environmental official who come to take the waste sample so
that they can carry out their technical task.

provisions.title: The sample of the hazardous waste which were taken during the
provisions.content:
monitoring or inspection shall be analysed in the Laboratory of the
Ministry of Environment.
The owner or person who responsible for the site stipulated in the
paragraph 1 of the article 23 of this sub-decree can request to test his/her
waste sample at other public or private laboratories which are recognized
formally and those laboratories must use the same testing method as used
in the laboratory of the Ministry of Environment.
The owner or person responsible for the point or site stipulated in the
paragraph 1 of the article 23 of this sub-decree shall pay analysis fee of
his/her owner waste sample following the list of testing cost determined
by the Minister of Environment and the Ministry of Economic and
Finance.
These incomes shall be incorporated into the national budget for
allocating the Environment Endowment Fund.

provisions.title: In the case of finding out that there are an illegal disposal or dumping
provisions.content:
of the hazardous waste without a permit from the competent institution.
The Ministry of Environment in collaboration with concerned ministries,
shall conduct the inspection at the places in complying with procedures as
follows:
a- To present his / her identity card and mission letter while,
entering into the premise or any site of point source of
pollution for conducting inspection and taking sample.
b- To make, at the site of inspection, the primary record and
report of the inspection or sample taking with the presence

-6-

25-JUL-2002 THU 14:33 P, 01

of witness if necessary;
c- To inquire and require the owner or responsible person of the
place to provide them with information and other relevant
documents for taking statement or report and for evidence;
d- To collect and detain evidence of the offence.

provisions.title: In case if there are complaint or report that there is storage or disposal
provisions.content:
of the hazardous waste which causes danger to animal or human health or
public property or contaminate the environment, the Ministry of
Environment shall make urgent inspection and inform concerned
ministries and local authority.

provisions.title: Violations of this Sub-Degree shall be fined and punished according to
provisions.content: Violations of this Sub-Degree shall be fined and punished according to

provisions.title: , 21, 22, 23 and article 25 of the chapter 9 of the Law on
provisions.content: Environment Protection and Natural Resource Management.

provisions.title: Any environmental inspection official or agent who is negligent fails
provisions.content:
to pay attention or conspires with a violator or facilities the commission of
a violation shall be subject to administrative sanction or face prosecution
before the court.

provisions.title: The report making and the prosecution for any person who violates or
provisions.content:
fails to abide by any article of this sub-decree is the responsibility of the
Ministry of Environment's official.

provisions.title: Any provision contrary to this sub-decree shall be considered null.
provisions.content: Any provision contrary to this sub-decree shall be considered null.

provisions.title: The minister in charge of the cabinet of the council of Ministers,
provisions.content:
concerned ministers and institutions shall collaborate with the Ministry of
Environment and implement this Sub- decree according to their duty.

provisions.title: This Sub-decree shall be in force from the date of its signature.
Licenses Sa Rom Trading Co Ltd, #122 Aeo, Street 430, Sangkat Toul
provisions.content:
Tum Puong, Khan Chamkarmon, represented by the company' s director
Mr. Yu Sa Rom, male, aged 29, Cambodian, holder of ID# 024215PP of
07 Sept 1992, to construct an industrial solid waste dump which is
located in Phum Chambok, Khan Kombol, Srok Angsnuol, Kandal
Province.

provisions.title: The company shall comply with the law on environmental protection and
provisions.content:
natural resource management, and the sub-decree on the environmental
impact assessment process.

provisions.title: The company shall comply with the approved environmental management
provisions.content: plan of the environmental impact assessment.

provisions.title: The company shall seek advice from the Ministry of Environment when it
provisions.content: changes the location or constructs a new division.

provisions.title: The company shall be obliged to donate environmental fund to the
provisions.content: Ministry of Environment until it ceases its operation.

provisions.title: Sa Rom Trading Co Ltd shall implement this Prakas.
provisions.content: Sa Rom Trading Co Ltd shall implement this Prakas.

provisions.title: Licenses Sa Rom Trading Co Ltd, #122 Aeo, Street 430, Sangkat Toul
provisions.content:
Tum Puong, Khan Chamkarmon, represented by the company' s director
Mr. Yu Sa Rom, male, aged 29, Cambodian, holder of ID# 024215PP of
07 Sept 1992, to construct an industrial solid waste dump and operate
waste collection and transportation business, as well as to monopolize the
storage of industrial waste in Phnom Penh and Kandal Province.

provisions.title: In constructing the industrial waste dump, the company shall be requierd
provisions.content:
to draw a plan and conduct plausibility study, and to report the
environmental impact assessment by outlining the clear environmental
preventive measures subject to the prior approval form the authority
concerned and land industry.

provisions.title: The company shall comply with the instructions from the institution in
provisions.content: charge during the course of construction.

provisions.title: During the operation of the industrial waste collection and transportation
provisions.content:
business, the company shall ensure public safety and the quality of the
environment around the waste dump, and shall write a monthly report
about the quantity of the collected waste and the waste storage, to the
Ministry of Environment.

provisions.title: Declares that the company shall be licensed to legally carry out this waste
provisions.content:
collection business for a period of 10 years, effective from the date of
signing on this Prakas.

provisions.title: Upon receiving the license, the company shall commence the construction
provisions.content:
process and business operation process immediately. In case the company
fails to commence construction within 1 year upon reception of the
license, this license shall be automatically confiscated.

provisions.title: In case the company breaks the law or seriously breaks any article
provisions.content:
stipulated above, this license shall be confiscated.

provisions.content:
Environmental pollution Control of the Ministry of Environment, Phnom
Penh Municipal Environmental Office, Kandal Environmental Office, and
Director of Sarom Trading Company shall implement this Prakas.

provisions.title: License Sarom Trading Co Ltd, at#122AEo, Street 430,
provisions.content:
Sangkat Toul Tum Puong, Khan Chamkarmon, to operate its solid waste
dumping site, which is located in Phum Kob Ambel, Khum Komboul,
Srok Angnuol, Kandal Province.

provisions.title: License Sarom Trading Co Ltd, at#122AEo, Street 430,Sangkat Toul Tum
provisions.content:
Puong, Khan Chamkarmon, and Municipal Waste Control Excise, located
at Municipal Office for Public Work and Transport, to collect and
transport industrial solid waste of enterprises/factories in Phnom Penh to
dump at Sarom Trading's dumping site.

provisions.title: The types of industrial solid waste inquired to be dump at Sarom Trading's
provisions.content: dumping site are specified in Annex 1 of this Prakas.

provisions.title: The companies specified in article 1 and 2 and the owners of
provisions.content:
enterprises/factories that use the service of the two company shall
implement the fee principle agreed upon at the 11 July 2002 meeting, of
which the details are giving in Annex 2 of this Prakas.

The principle of service fees is subject to review every six months
providing the service provider of service consumer lodges a request to the
Ministry of Environment.

provisions.title: The companies providing collecting and transporting service shall ensure
provisions.content:
that the means to collect and transport the solid waste from factories to
Sarom Trading's dumping site do not cause harm to the public safety and
environment. In addition, the collection and transportation methods shall
fulfil the technical requirement determined by the institution in charge.

provisions.title: In case the service provided by two companies does not meet demands of
provisions.content:
the enterprises/factories, or fails to provide service for a certain reason,
owners of factories can lodge a request to the Ministry of Environment to
collect and transport the solid waste specified in Annex 1 to dump at
Sarom Trading's dumping site on a temporary or permanent basis, as
proved by the Ministry of Environment.

provisions.title: The collection and transportation of the solid waste specified in Article 3
provisions.content:
to be dumped at Sarom Trading's dumping shall require the factory
owner's seeking approval from the Ministry of Environment.

The collecting and transporting companies and the Sarom Trading's dumping site
shall forward a monthly report to the Ministry of Environment stating:
a. the types and volume of solid waste collected or dumped
b. the original source of solid waste
c. the methods of wrapping and transporting or storing

- 13 -

25-JUL-2002 THU 14:33 P, 01

provisions.title: Sarom Trading Company shall be responsible for properly managing its
provisions.content:
own dumping site so as to protect the public safety and the surrounding
environment, and shall implement the instructions from the officials in
charge.

provisions.title: The Ministry of Environment shall confiscate the licence from the two
provisions.content:
companies should they break the law or seriously breach any article in the
Prakas.

provisions.title: Any decision contradictory to this Prakas shall be considered invalid.
provisions.content: Any decision contradictory to this Prakas shall be considered invalid.

provisions.title: Director General of the Technical Department, Director of the
provisions.content:
Environmental Pollution Control of the Ministry of Environment, Phnom
Penh Municipal Environmental Office, Kandal Environment Office, and
Director of Sarom Trading Company, and Director of Municipal Waste
Control Excise shall implement this Prakas.

provisions.title: This Prakas shall come into force from the date on which it is signed.

ANNEX 1
TYPES OF INDUSTRIAL SOLID WASTE REQUIRED TO BE DUMPED AT
SAROM
TRADING'S DUMPING SITE

- 14 -

25-JUL-2002 THU 14:33 P, 01

1. Semi-dry muddy waste removed from effluent treatment pool
2. Coloured fibres and pieces of fabrics
3. Plastic waste containing Polyvinyl Chloride
4. Rubber waste and vulcanized rubber containing Polymer-Butilin
5. Batteries and battery waste
6. Ashes left over after the burning of medical waste
7. Lighting waste and electrical devices
8. Paint waste. lacquer and packaged materials
9. Insecticide waste used in agriculture and in packaged materials (must be placed in
a rubber tub before being dumped)
10. Waste resulted from production and utilization of printing ink
11. Expired goods or goods that do not meet required standard
12. Negatives
13. Expired medicine and waste resulted from production of medicine
14. Waste containing Asbestos substance

ANNEX 2
TRANSPORTATION FEES AND DUMPING FEES AT
SAROM TRADING' DEMPING SITE
1. The fee for transporting ONE cubic meter of semi-dry muddy waste from the
factory to the dumping site is 10 USD (10 USD/m³). This fee also
INCLUDES the labor employed to load the waste onto and unload the waste
from the vehicle. The fee shall be settled once a month.
2. The fee for dumping ONE cubic meter of mud waste at Sarom Trading's
dumping site is 3 USD (3 USD/m³). The fee will be charged EACH TIME the
waste is dumped.
3. Monthly fees for transporting the solid waste by a vehicle of 5m³ capacity are
as follows:
• 60 USD for 4 to 6 time of transporting/month
• 72 USD for 8 to 10 time of transporting/month
• 90 USD for 12 times for transporting/month
• 150 USD per month if the transportation takes place ONCE a day
• 270 USD per month if the transportation takes place TWICE a day
• 240 USD per month if the transportation takes place THRICE a day

- 15 -

25-JUL-2002 THU 14:33 P, 01

4. The fee for dumping ONE cubic meter of solid waste specified in points 2, 3,4
and 7 of ANNEX 1 is 1 USD (USD/m³). The fee will be charged EACH
TIME the waste is dumped.
5. The fee for dumping solid waste, which are not specified in points 1 & 4 of
ANNEX 2, shall be determined by the agreement the waste owner and the
dumping site owner.

- 16 -
